design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Panel event-study calendar-alias Smith-normal-form certificate. Pre-anchor: closest published anchors are Goodman-Bacon/Sun-Abraham/Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess event-study decomposition and contamination results; closest banked attempts are q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov and panel_jackknife_signflip_phase_v1. Our theorem is not that because it is not another negative-weight, jackknife, or generic rank-perturbation statement: the focal object is a finite integer/Fourier alias certificate for calendar-residue adoption. Hand object: four adoption residues r=0,1,2,3, event bins k=-1,0,1,2, and the written table H[r,k]=(-1)^(r+k), so rows alternate [-,+,-,+] and [+,-,+,-]; the claimed certificate is the Smith-normal-form/Fourier alias vector showing exactly when a period-2 calendar component is indistinguishable from an alternating event-time contrast after the panel incidence map. Non-trivial because the certificate is a primitive incidence-table obstruction, not a support/overlap or LP/Farkas bound. Reject if it collapses to standard TWFE contamination, routine rank algebra, Q1 spectral reuse, or a definition-unfold frontier.

proposed_from.topic: Panel event-study calendar-alias Smith-normal-form certificate. Pre-anchor: closest published anchors are Goodman-Bacon/Sun-Abraham/Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess event-study decomposition and contamination results; closest banked attempts are q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov and panel_jackknife_signflip_phase_v1. Our theorem is not that because it is not another negative-weight, jackknife, or generic rank-perturbation statement: the focal object is a finite integer/Fourier alias certificate for calendar-residue adoption. Hand object: four adoption residues r=0,1,2,3, event bins k=-1,0,1,2, and the written table H[r,k]=(-1)^(r+k), so rows alternate [-,+,-,+] and [+,-,+,-]; the claimed certificate is the Smith-normal-form/Fourier alias vector showing exactly when a period-2 calendar component is indistinguishable from an alternating event-time contrast after the panel incidence map. Non-trivial because the certificate is a primitive incidence-table obstruction, not a support/overlap or LP/Farkas bound. Reject if it collapses to standard TWFE contamination, routine rank algebra, Q1 spectral reuse, or a definition-unfold frontier.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue comparators are q1_spectral_phase_transition/p1_markov, panel_jackknife_signflip_phase/v1, and eid_bjs_efficient_event_study/v1 reviewer notes. The gap is that those proposals concern Markov row-space visibility, deletion/sign-flip behavior, and estimator-equivalence active-rank diagnostics, not a primitive Smith-normal-form/Fourier residue frontier over calendar residues, event windows, omitted bins, and adoption support.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are SunAbraham2021EventStudies, BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024Revisiting, SchmidheinySiegloch2023EventStudies, GoodmanBacon2021Timing, CallawaySantAnna2021MultiplePeriods, and DeChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020TWFE. They provide contamination, imputation, binning equivalence, timing-weight, aggregation, and nonconvex-weight results; none states an integer invariant-factor or finite Fourier character certificate for calendar-residue aliasing of alternating event-time contrasts."}
seed_list: Construct a Smith-normal-form iff certificate deciding when a period-two calendar component is indistinguishable from an alternating event-time contrast in a finite residue event-study incidence map.
Classify the residue-modulus/event-window frontier where the four-residue alias persists as an invariant obstruction rather than a single hand table.
Define an omitted-bin-invariant active-rank diagnostic for whether an alternating event-time contrast is estimable before treatment effects are estimated.
Build an alias-module projection estimator that drops certified calendar aliases and gives root-n inference for the surviving event contrasts.
Give a finite Fourier character criterion equivalent to the SNF certificate for calendar-residue event-study aliasing.
Construct the minimal non-degenerate residue family in which BJS-style imputation has a calendar-alias non-identification direction unrelated to forbidden comparisons.
Characterize when event-study binning creates or destroys period-two alias certificates across Schmidheiny-Siegloch distributed-lag equivalence classes.
Separate calendar-residue aliasing from Goodman-Bacon timing weights by proving the same alias module can occur with all timing-comparison weights nonnegative.
Define a normalization-invariant contrast audit whose value is unchanged by the omitted event bin and by calendar fixed-effect normalization.
Promote the four-by-four hand table to an open support class of balanced residue panels with the same primitive integer alias vector.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a Smith-normal-form iff certificate deciding when a period-two calendar component is indistinguishable from an alternating event-time contrast in a finite residue event-study incidence map.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal eid_switchback_debruijn_carryover
anchor_paper.claim: Prior incidence-map alias work returned an alias delta but collapsed as routine row-space machinery.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: schedule-audit map returns an identifying formula ... or otherwise returns an alias delta

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends row-space aliasing, but the non-trivial content is a primitive integer/Fourier certificate with SNF invariant factors.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from standard rank-nullity because any collinearity is a kernel vector.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap asks for the primitive integer residue kernel with event and calendar projections fixed, not existence of a real null vector.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not eid_switchback_debruijn_carryover_v1 because the focal object is the SNF/Fourier residue certificate, not a generic row-space alias delta.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine rank algebra is upgraded to a named primitive integer certificate and an iff estimability statement.

adversarial_referee.instance: Panel theorem may lack estimator-facing content.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Add Conjecture 2 alias-module projection estimator and root-n/non-identification split.

adversarial_referee.instance: Four-by-four table may be only a toy witness.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 1 promotes it to a finite family iff over m,K,R.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims M7 but proof might only show sufficiency.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State iff as conjecture with necessity via quotient-module counterexample route.

seed_details.one_line: Classify the residue-modulus/event-window frontier where the four-residue alias persists as an invariant obstruction rather than a single hand table.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair
anchor_paper.claim: A prior finite alias witness failed because it lacked a hard theorem beyond the witness.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: finite graph-pair exposure aliasing sounded concrete but lacked a hard theorem beyond the witness

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample-search seed is stronger because it targets a family frontier and not a single finite witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the four-by-four example with variables renamed.
seed_details.rebuttal: The deliverable is the exact family frontier over moduli, bins, omitted bins, and residue support.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair_v1 because it promotes the witness to an explicit residue-family frontier.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the new object is the family frontier of primitive integer aliases, not a generic rank criterion.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could still rely on one hand table.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Requires open family statement in Conjecture 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Frontier may prove only alias sufficiency.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Necessity is routed through SNF quotient structure.

adversarial_referee.instance: Binning equivalence papers may already imply it.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Schmidheiny-Siegloch gives equivalence, not integer residue invariant factors.

seed_details.one_line: Define an omitted-bin-invariant active-rank diagnostic for whether an alternating event-time contrast is estimable before treatment effects are estimated.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal eid_bjs_efficient_event_study
anchor_paper.claim: A reviewer requested a fully observable active-rank object for event-study equivalence.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Replace the tau-dependent H_pre diagnostic with a fully observable ... active-rank object

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weak active-rank reuse is avoided by making the diagnostic an omitted-bin-invariant SNF module.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is exactly the active-rank request from the prior reviewer.
seed_details.rebuttal: The prior request named a gap; this seed fills it with a specific integer/Fourier object and alias/nonalias estimator split.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1 because the object is a normalization-invariant SNF alias module, not a tau-dependent rank diagnostic.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the diagnostic is a computable integer module with invariant factors.

adversarial_referee.instance: BJS targets imputation efficiency, not alias diagnostics.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use BJS only as collinearity comparator; the prior-review anchor supplies active-rank gap.

adversarial_referee.instance: Diagnostic without estimator may cap at field.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Pair diagnostic with projection estimator in Conjecture 2.

adversarial_referee.instance: May only certify failure, not estimability.

seed_details.one_line: Build an alias-module projection estimator that drops certified calendar aliases and gives root-n inference for the surviving event contrasts.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal q1_spectral_phase_transition
anchor_paper.claim: The prior spectral threshold is a different object and not a comparison decomposition.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: different object: a spectral support threshold ... not a comparison decomposition

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Panel estimation-only risk is reduced by pairing estimator with the SNF identification certificate.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is routine projection after computing any nullspace.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nonroutine part is which rational projection is invariant to omitted-bin and calendar normalization choices.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov because the certificate is integer/Fourier residue aliasing, not spectral support visibility.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: projection acts on the SNF alias module and is tied to the estimability theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: Estimator could be a postscript to identification.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 2 makes inference part of the result.
Frame estimator for any contrast orthogonal to alias module.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims M9 but no rate details.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State root-n normality under finite-cell sampling regularity.

seed_details.one_line: Give a finite Fourier character criterion equivalent to the SNF certificate for calendar-residue event-study aliasing.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal q1_spectral_phase_transition
anchor_paper.claim: The closest spectral proposal concerns support thresholds rather than residue Fourier aliases.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: different object: a spectral support threshold ... not a comparison decomposition

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-spectral risk is addressed by using finite characters only as the dual form of the integer certificate.
seed_details.referee_objection: Fourier diagonalization is standard for cyclic groups.
seed_details.rebuttal: Standard diagonalization does not by itself identify the event-calendar alias vector in the normalized panel incidence quotient.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov because it uses finite Fourier characters to certify integer aliases, not spectral phase transitions.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless paired with the SNF iff theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Construct the minimal non-degenerate residue family in which BJS-style imputation has a calendar-alias non-identification direction unrelated to forbidden comparisons.

anchor_paper.author: Kirill Borusyak, Xavier Jaravel, and Jann Spiess
anchor_paper.claim: BJS robust event-study imputation handles forbidden comparisons and collinearity but lacks an integer alias certificate.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample risk is controlled by requiring a non-degenerate residue family, not one hand-tuned law.
seed_details.referee_objection: BJS already discusses collinearity, so this is a relabelling.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed obstruction is an integer residue alias with an explicit certificate, not the imputation estimator's collinearity condition.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize when event-study binning creates or destroys period-two alias certificates across Schmidheiny-Siegloch distributed-lag equivalence classes.

anchor_paper.author: Kurt Schmidheiny and Sebastian Siegloch
anchor_paper.claim: Binning and distributed-lag equivalence give a design-matrix anchor but not a residue-family alias frontier.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: T=2-to-general-T risk is avoided by changing the binning regime and tracking integer invariant factors.
seed_details.rebuttal: Distributed-lag equivalence does not classify period-two calendar aliases under omitted-bin normalization.

seed_details.one_line: Separate calendar-residue aliasing from Goodman-Bacon timing weights by proving the same alias module can occur with all timing-comparison weights nonnegative.

anchor_paper.author: Andrew Goodman-Bacon
anchor_paper.claim: The decomposition exposes timing-comparison weights but not normalization-invariant event-calendar aliases.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Negative-weight anti-pattern is avoided by explicitly making the alias coexist with nonnegative timing weights.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is still a negative-weight paper in disguise.
seed_details.rebuttal: The theorem would exhibit aliasing even when the Goodman-Bacon diagnostic is silent.

seed_details.one_line: Define a normalization-invariant contrast audit whose value is unchanged by the omitted event bin and by calendar fixed-effect normalization.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal eid_bjs_efficient_event_study
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewer asked for a fully observable active-rank object.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Replace the tau-dependent H_pre diagnostic with a fully observable ... active-rank object

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Omitted-bin normalization diagnostic is stronger if tied to SNF rather than rank alone.
seed_details.referee_objection: Normalization invariance is just linear algebra bookkeeping.
seed_details.rebuttal: Bookkeeping becomes publishable only when it yields the primitive alias certificate; alone this is field-tier.

seed_details.one_line: Promote the four-by-four hand table to an open support class of balanced residue panels with the same primitive integer alias vector.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair
anchor_paper.claim: A prior aliasing witness failed for lacking a theorem beyond the witness.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: finite graph-pair exposure aliasing sounded concrete but lacked a hard theorem beyond the witness

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Single-witness risk remains; useful only as support for the stronger family certificate.
seed_details.referee_objection: Open support class is a thin generic-class promotion.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is not chosen as main seed; the stronger SNF iff statement carries the proposal.

proposed_from.literature_map: Goodman-Bacon (2021, Journal of Econometrics) decomposes TWFE DiD with timing variation into weighted two-by-two comparisons. Sun-Abraham (2021, Journal of Econometrics) shows staggered event-study leads/lags can be contaminated by other relative-period effects. Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess (2024, Review of Economic Studies) gives robust imputation event-study estimation and treats calendar/event-time collinearity as an identification issue. de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER) shows TWFE can recover nonconvex weighted averages under heterogeneous effects. Callaway-Sant'Anna (2021, Journal of Econometrics) identifies group-time ATT parameters and supports event-time, group, and calendar aggregation. Schmidheiny-Siegloch (2023, Journal of Applied Econometrics) analyzes event-study binning and distributed-lag equivalence. Freyaldenhoven-Hansen-Shapiro (2019, AER) addresses unobserved confounding tied to policy timing using pre-event trends/proxies. Roth-Sant'Anna-Bilinski-Poe (2023, Journal of Econometrics) surveys modern DiD issues around timing, parallel trends, and inference. The Stage -1.1 scan found contamination, weighting, aggregation, collinearity, and binning anchors, but no published SNF, invariant-factor, or finite-Fourier alias certificate for calendar-residue event-study incidence. Prior-proposal banks warn that generic row-space, spectral, single-witness, and sign-flip framings collapse to routine machinery.
